title: Why didnt MAGI Selfdestruct
wikitext:
[]: Why didn’t MAGI Selfdestruct?             Quote Post by Gendos Glasses » Fri Jul 19, 2024 8:55 am Hello there I binge watched all episodes and EoE on Netflix and was a bit perplexed about the final confrontation between Kitsaro and Gendo. Kitsaro said she uploaded a program for self destruct but one of MAGI parts (I think The Woman, Casper) was against it. If I understood the magi correct, they decide every decision per majority rule. So how did Casper overrule the decision with only one vote?

[]: Re: Why didn’t MAGI Selfdestruct?             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Jul 19, 2024 1:29 pm Something like Self-Destruct almost definitely requires a consensus.

[]: Re: Why didn’t MAGI Selfdestruct?             Quote Post by KMG » Fri Oct 04, 2024 11:22 am For some decisions, majority vote of 2/3 is sufficient enough for the magi to uphold a decision. If I recall correctly, for more drastic decisions, such as the self destruction of Nerv HQ, a unanimous vote of all 3 Magi has to take place. In the episode in question, an angel is attempting to trigger a self destruct and by this point had only managed to take control of 2 of the Magi. Additionally, to clear any confusion, Ritsuko wasn't uploading a program to trigger a self destruct command for the Magi, she was writing code that would put the angel in a logical stalemate; to self destruct or co-exist. That is, the self destruction of the angel, not Nerv HQ. tl:dr; Ritsuko was guiding the Angel to its own death, not the Magi. Magi have to all agree for decisions such as self destruct.

[]: Re: Why didn’t MAGI Selfdestruct?             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Wed Oct 09, 2024 11:02 am KMG wrote: In the episode in question, an angel is attempting to trigger a self destruct and by this point had only managed to take control of 2 of the Magi. Additionally, to clear any confusion, Ritsuko wasn't uploading a program to trigger a self destruct command for the Magi, she was writing code that would put the angel in a logical stalemate; to self destruct or co-exist. That is, the self destruction of the angel, not Nerv HQ.

[]: Re: Why didn’t MAGI Selfdestruct?             Quote Post by brandneweyes » Tue Dec 03, 2024 4:14 pm If I'm not mistaken. Ritsuko's philosophy according to her (I'm paraphrasing): with enough science you can tame the human heart. Mother & Scientist agreed, but in an ironic twist of fate concerning Ritsuko, Naoko's love (Woman) for Gendo prevented Magi from making the same decision. I.e. You cannot tame the human heart (i.e love) with science.
